query: Meghalaya Rajya Sabha election 2026 candidates nominations filed
answer:
## Meghalaya Rajya Sabha Election 2026 — Research Report

### Election Date
**June 18, 2026** — announced by the Election Commission of India on May 22, 2026. Polling will take place between 9 AM and 4 PM, with counting at 5 PM the same day. Meghalaya has **one Rajya Sabha seat** up for election.

---

### Retiring Member
**Dr. Wanweiroy Kharlukhi (WR Kharlukhi)** — National People's Party (NPP)
- Assumed office: June 22, 2020
- Won the 2020 election defeating Congress candidate Kennedy Khyriem by 20 votes (39 out of 59 votes polled)
- His 6-year term expires in 2026, triggering this election

---

### MDA-II Coalition Candidate (Ruling Alliance)
**James PK Sangma** — National People's Party (NPP)

- Announced on **June 3, 2026** as the official MDA-II candidate
- Decision made at an MDA-II meeting chaired by **Chief Minister Conrad K. Sangma**
- **Expected to file nomination papers on June 5, 2026 at 2:30 PM**
- James PK Sangma is the **brother of CM Conrad Sangma** and **son of the late P.A. Sangma** (former Lok Sabha Speaker, NPP founder)
- Currently serves as **National Working President of NPP** (since March 2025)
- Former Rajya Sabha MP (2008–2014), former Meghalaya Cabinet Minister (2018–2021)
- Lost his assembly seat in the 2023 elections

---

### Assembly Composition (The Electorate)
The 60-member Meghalaya Legislative Assembly:

| Bloc | Party | Seats |
|------|-------|-------|
| **MDA-II (Govt)** | NPP | 33 |
| | UDP | 12 |
| | BJP | 2 |
| | HSPDP | 2 |
| | Independents | 2 |
| **Total Govt** | | **51** |
| **Opposition** | AITC (TMC) | 5 |
| | VPP | 4 |
| **Total Opposition** | | **9** |

With 51 of 60 seats, the MDA-II alliance has an overwhelming majority — only 31 votes are needed to win the lone seat.

---

### Opposition Candidates
As of June 3, 2026, **no opposition candidate has been publicly announced**. Given the ruling alliance's 51-seat strength, any opposition candidacy would be largely symbolic.

---

### Key Timeline
| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| May 22, 2026 | ECI announces election date |
| June 3, 2026 | MDA-II announces James PK Sangma as candidate |
| June 5, 2026 | Expected nomination filing (2:30 PM) |
| June 18, 2026 | Polling day |
| June 20, 2026 | Process completion deadline |

key_facts: Meghalaya Rajya Sabha election 2026 is scheduled for June 18, 2026, as announced by the Election Commission of India on May 22, 2026.
Only one Rajya Sabha seat from Meghalaya is up for election.
The retiring member is Dr. Wanweiroy Kharlukhi (NPP), who was elected in June 2020 for a 6-year term.
The MDA-II ruling alliance announced James PK Sangma (NPP) as their candidate on June 3, 2026.
James PK Sangma is expected to file his nomination papers on June 5, 2026 at 2:30 PM.
James PK Sangma is the brother of Chief Minister Conrad Sangma and son of the late P.A. Sangma (former Lok Sabha Speaker and NPP founder).
James PK Sangma currently serves as National Working President of NPP and is a former Rajya Sabha MP (2008-2014).
The MDA-II coalition controls 51 of 60 assembly seats (NPP 33, UDP 12, BJP 2, HSPDP 2, Independents 2), making the outcome virtually certain.
The opposition holds only 9 seats (AITC 5, VPP 4) and has not yet announced a candidate as of June 3, 2026.
In the 2020 Rajya Sabha election for this seat, NPP's Kharlukhi defeated Congress's Kennedy Khyriem by 20 votes (39-19).
